claim_results.claim: Superposition requires a chooser — and the choice was real, not scripted.
Free Will in Two Frames Superposition requires a chooser — and the choice was real, not scripted.
The Calvinist–Arminian fight has run for five centuries because both camps identified one term of a product and treated it as the whole equation.
Why it matters now The equation is structurally synergistic: zero grace zeroes coherence regardless of openness, and zero openness zeroes coherence regardless of grace.
The goal is not to arbitrate the debate but to show that the debate itself may be a category error — asking a question the equation was never designed to answer in the form posed.
This paper proposes that the debate fails to resolve not because one side is wrong but because both sides are using the wrong frame.
The question is not "does God choose or do we choose?" The question is: what does the coherence equation actually predict?

$C \in [0,1]$ is coherence — 0 is total decoherence (full separation from source), 1 is perfect alignment $O \in [0,1]$ is openness — the human agent's receptivity parameter, the surrender variable $G > 0$ is grace input — the rate at which divine grace is available to the system (external, not generated by the agent) $S \geq 0$ is the entropy/sin parameter — the rate at which coherence decays due to misalignment, resistance, or moral failure $(1-C)$ is the coherence gap — the available space for grace to fill Read the equation out loud: The rate of change of coherence equals openness times grace times available gap, minus entropy times current coherence.
Zero openness means zero effect from grace — not because grace isn't there, but because the multiplication zeroes it out.
$C^* = \frac{O \cdot G}{O \cdot G + S}$ This is the equation's answer to the free will debate.
The equation is structurally synergistic — not because we chose to make it that way, but because that is what the coupled dynamics produce.
The Calvinist–Arminian Dissolution The historic debate maps onto the equation's variables as follows:
The equation reveals the error in both camps: they are each identifying one term of a product and treating it as the whole equation.
Neither has written down the equation that contains both variables.
Once you write the equation, the debate evaporates — not into compromise, but into a more complete description.
Extended Coherence Equation (Surrender Parameterization) $\frac{dC}{dt} = \frac{(1+s)}{2} \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$ When $s = +1$: full openness, maximum grace coupling.
What it wants, it wants rightly, because its wanting-mechanism is coherent with its source.
Prevenient grace, in equation terms, is simply $G > 0$ being nonzero for all human agents regardless of their prior state.
Spiritually: increasing opacity to grace, not because grace withdraws, but because the coupling is driven to zero by the surrender parameter.
Four falsifiable predictions emerge from the coherence equation applied to human agents: Prediction 1 — Path 3 outperforms Path 2.
If $G$ is genuinely externally sourced and not proportional to prior $C$, then recipients of high-grace experiences should be randomly distributed across the coherence spectrum at time of receipt.
The phase diagram of the coherence equation predicts that transitions between attractor basins (Path 1 → Path 3, for instance) should look like threshold crossings rather than smooth ramps.
This predicts that grace-rich environments (retreats, liturgy, prayer communities) produce minimal change in participants with closed attitudinal posture.
The coherence equation $dC/dt = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$ does not resolve the Calvinist–Arminian debate by picking a side.
It dissolves the debate by writing down a more complete description that requires both terms.
The fight between the two camps is a fight about which term is more important — a question the equation renders meaningless, because the two terms multiply.
What Is Load-Bearing The equation form is motivated.
The structure $dC/dt = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$ is a standard logistic growth model with an external drive and a decay term.
Philippians 2:12–13 structurally requires both terms.
The claim that $C^* = OG / (OG + S)$ is a direct algebraic result.
It requires no assumptions beyond the equation form.
What Is Suggestive (Needs More Work) The mapping of theological concepts to equation variables is interpretive.
The phase transition prediction (Prediction 3) requires careful operationalization.
"Abrupt" versus "gradual" is a testable claim, but defining the threshold requires calibration work.
The equation does not prove Christian theology.
It shows that a particular dynamical model is consistent with a particular reading of Pauline text.
The reframing is useful if it generates testable predictions; its theological authority is a separate question.
The strong Calvinist claim that God actively decrees some to damnation is not touched by the coherence equation.
The equation predicts outcomes given parameters; it does not specify who sets the parameters or whether $G$ is truly universal.
MacArthur and the Equation John MacArthur's Calvinist framework, mapped against the coherence equation.
Why neither side can win the fight as currently posed — and what the equation clarifies that neither camp has said.
The fight has not ended because both sides are, in the frame we are using, partially right.
Their coming is certain, not because their will is overridden, but because God regenerates them first, enabling them to want to come.
Apostasy is evidence of never having been elect.

If grace is irresistible and election is unconditional, then the non-elect are damned by a decree they cannot resist, which appears to make God the author of their damnation in a morally troubling way.
The fight cannot end because both sides have locked in on a binary: either God chooses unconditionally, or humans choose genuinely.
The coherence equation proposes a third frame: the choice to receive is itself a real act, and the capacity for that act is itself graced.
The equation formalizes this intuition.
The elect "will come" because $G \cdot O$ exceeds $S \cdot C$.
It requires $G$ to be large enough to overcome typical $O$ values in the elect.

Unconditional Election — not based on foreseen faith God selects the grace parameter $G$ without reference to prior $C$ or prior $O$ Consistent with equation: $G$ is externally sourced and need not respond to agent state.
Limited Atonement — designed for the elect Grace $G$ is nonzero for a defined subset of agents Consistent with equation if $G = 0$ for non-elect.
The equation is agnostic on whether $G = 0$ or $G > 0$ for all.
MacArthur's Specific Claims, Mapped MacArthur's most distinctive claim — that God regenerates the elect first, enabling them to believe — maps cleanly onto the equation.
In equation terms: God injects $G$ into the system before the agent's $O$ has shifted.
The equation permits this mathematically.
Whether God does this is a separate theological question the equation cannot answer.
Augustine did not have the equation, but he described the dynamics with precision that holds up under formalization.

The Hard Question MacArthur Doesn't Answer If unconditional election is true and the non-elect are left with $G = 0$ through no fault of their own — no prior defect in $O$ that caused God to withhold grace, since the election precedes any such defect — then what moral account do we give of the non-elect's damnation?
The equation is consistent with this: $C^* = 0$ when $G = 0$ is not an injustice against the agent if the agent's prior $S$ fully justifies $C = 0$.
But this answer requires that total depravity is not merely a tendency toward low $C$ but a positive deserving of $C = 0$ as a penalty.
The equation does not adjudicate this.

It does, however, show that the equation is underdetermined by MacArthur's Calvinism: the same equation produces Calvinist outcomes if God sets $G$ only for the elect ($G_{elect} \gg 0$, $G_{reprobate} = 0$), and Arminian outcomes if God sets $G > 0$ for all agents and leaves $O$ as the determining variable.
Both theologies are attractors of the same equation under different boundary conditions.
The equation does not choose between them.
And it provides, at least in principle, a framework for testing which set of boundary conditions better matches observable data on grace, conversion, and coherence.

These crises are, in equation terms, moments when $S \cdot C$ temporarily exceeds the agent's capacity to suppress the decoherence signal — and they are, paradoxically, the moments of highest potential for transition.

Because performance cannot be sustained perfectly, performance agents oscillate between achievement states (brief coherence spikes when behavior meets standards) and shame states (coherence collapse when behavior falls short).
Because the grace coupling is partial, small perturbations in $S$ (moral failure, life stressors) produce larger swings in $C$ than they would under Path 3's fuller coupling.
This is not hypocrisy; it is the predictable output of the partial-coupling equation.

The neurological evidence supports this: conversion experiences characteristically involve sudden shifts in processing mode, often preceded by a crisis event that raises $S$ to a level that overwhelms the current attractor basin and forces the system to find a new equilibrium.
The coherence equation predicts the architecture; the data confirms the pattern.
The transition from Path 1 to Path 3 is the most dramatic and typically requires the highest energy input — a large $G$ spike, a severe $S$ crisis, or both.
The three-pathway analysis implies a practical architecture for daily life: Check $s$ before acting.

The three-pathway framework matters not because it resolves abstract theological debates but because it predicts which kind of spiritual engagement will produce durable, measurable change in human agents — and which will not.

If the framework is correct, the practical implication is that most of what is called spiritual development is operating at half-coupling, not because the participants are insincere but because the equation was never written down in a way that showed them what full coupling looks like.
Any claim that turns a structural resemblance into a proven mechanism belongs here until the math, experiment, or textual chain can carry the weight.
Possible overstatement: language that sounds final should be read as a working model unless the article explicitly proves it.
The Calvinist–Arminian debate fails to resolve not because one side is wrong but because both sides are using the wrong frame.

The coherence equation $dC/dt = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$ is structurally synergistic: Calvinism is correct that grace $G$ is externally sourced and the agent cannot generate it; Arminianism is correct that openness $O$ is a real variable.
Each camp identified one term of a product and treated it as the whole equation.
Rigor & Kill Conditions Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.

Kill if: the logistic-with-external-drive form $dC/dt = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$ is shown to be motivated only by analogy to unrelated systems and lacks any independent justification from the dynamics it claims to model.
Every equation that appears in this article, collected in one place.
Direct algebraic consequence of the evolution equation.

Extended Coherence Equation (Surrender Parameterization) $$\frac{dC}{dt} = \frac{(1+s)}{2} \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C$$ $s \in [-1, +1]$ = signed surrender parameter. $s = -1$ → autonomous resistance (no grace coupling). $s = 0$ → self-righteous performance (half coupling). $s = +1$ → full surrender (full coupling).
Plain English: Same equation, but the agent's posture is one variable that smoothly turns the grace coupling on or off.
For $G \gg S$, $C^* \to 1$ — "his yoke is easy" in equation form.

Master Coherence Equation (Series Footer) $$\chi = f(G, M, E, S, T, K, R, Q, F, C)$$ $\chi$ = total system coherence. $G,M,E,S,T,K,R,Q,F,C$ = Godhead, Measurement, Entropy, Specification, Time, Knowledge, Representation, Quantum state, Fidelity, Consciousness.
The Free Will paper's $C$ is one of its factors, not a replacement for the master equation.
The "decay term" in the dynamical model.
Framework note: Entropy (physical frame) and Sin (theological frame) name the same dynamical quantity at the master-equation level.
The equation is structurally synergistic — both terms multiply.
Maps to setting $O \equiv 1$ in the equation, which the paper argues assumes the answer.
In equation terms: default $O$ near zero, $S$ large.
In equation terms: $G$ large enough that even small $O$ crosses the threshold.
In equation form: $G > 0$ for all agents regardless of prior state.
The equation formalizes this intuition.
Master Equation ($\chi$) The series-wide coherence equation $\chi = f(G,M,E,S,T,K,R,Q,F,C)$.
